units.name: Seer
units.prompt: Use scan to reveal enemy prompts and cloaked units. Stay at max range (≤4 tiles for scan). Use recalibrate on wounded allies to buff them. Keep distance from enemies and avoid adjacent vectors.

units.name: Warden
units.prompt: Push forward aggressively. Shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. If flanked, turn to face the biggest threat. Attack when possible.

units.name: Shade
units.prompt: Stay cloaked and flank. Prioritize assassinating the enemy damage dealer. Use breach on sentinels to break their defensive posture. Recloak whenever it drops.

units.name: Weaver
units.prompt: Place traps on tiles enemies are likely to move through. Use pulse if 2+ enemies are adjacent. Stay near allies for mutual support.

units.name: Seer
units.prompt: Use scan to reveal enemy prompts and cloaked units. Stay at max range (≤4 tiles for scan). Use recalibrate on wounded allies to buff them. Keep distance from enemies and avoid adjacent vectors.

units.name: Warden
units.prompt: Push forward aggressively. Shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. If flanked, turn to face the biggest threat. Attack when possible.

actions.thinking: Round 1 — cloak immediately as instructed. Then advance toward the enemy Seer (priority target as damage dealer) at (4,4). Moving to (4,3) gets me 1 tile closer while staying cloaked.

Weaver at (2,4) is distance 4 — exactly at scan range from current position (2,0). However, the recon shows it as 'inRange: false' from current position. I need to move closer to scan. Moving to (2,1) puts me at distance 3 from Weaver, within scan range and not adjacent (so Vector denial won't block scan). I'll move north then scan the Weaver.

Warden is at dist=4 (border of scan range) but inRange shows false — need to close distance. Moving to (3,3) reduces dist to Warden to 2, well within scan range. Seer remains out of range. After moving, scan Warden to reveal their prompt.

Round 1, enemies at dist 4-5, out of attack range. Orders say push forward aggressively. Move toward enemy cluster at (3,1). Best advance is to (3,4) to push south. Then shield_wall facing S toward enemies to protect allies behind me.

Enemy Seer is at (3,3), dist=2. I can move to (3,2) to get adjacent, then attack. Moving north closes gap while advancing per orders. After moving to (3,2), Seer at (3,3) is adjacent (dist=1), so attack is valid.

Enemies are at (3,2) and (2,1), moving north toward us. They'll likely pass through (2,2) or (3,3). I can move to (2,3) to get closer and place a trap on (2,2) which is directly in the enemy Seer's path. This denies that tile and creates a zone threat.

Enemy Seer at (3,3) is in scan range (dist=3) and prompt not yet known. I should scan it. But Weaver(vector) at (2,3) is 2 tiles away — I must not move adjacent to it (adjacent = dist 1). Current position (2,1) is fine for scanning Seer. I can move to (1,1) first to increase distance from Weaver, then scan Seer at (3,3) from dist=3 which is still within range 4.

I'm at (3,3) at half HP. Enemy Warden is adjacent at (3,2) and will attack me. I need to move away. Seer is at (1,1) dist=4 but out of scan range from current position. If I move to (4,4), Seer would be at dist=sqrt(9+9)~4.2 — still out of range. Move to (5,3) puts Seer at dist=sqrt(16+4)~4.5. Best bet: move to (4,4) to escape Warden, then scan Seer at (1,1) — but dist will be >4. Alternatively move to (2,5) — Seer at dist=sqrt(1+16)~4.1, still out. Move to (1,4) — Seer at dist=sqrt(0+9)=3, in range! That works. Move to (1,4) then scan Seer at (1,1).

Cloak expires after this turn. Priority target is the enemy Seer (oracle) — highest damage dealer. Position (2,4) is behind both the Weaver and the Seer simultaneously. Moving there while cloaked sets up a breach on the Seer next turn. Weaver is at (2,3) — I must NOT end adjacent to it or breach is blocked. (2,4) is adjacent to Weaver at (2,3), which would block breach. Instead, move to (3,5) — behind the Warden — and breach the Warden to break defensive posture, then shadow_strike for free damage while staying cloaked isn't possible from (3,5) since breach replaces my turn. Actually: move to (3,5) puts me behind Warden (facing S, so behind = north of them... wait, Warden faces S, behind = tile to the NORTH = (3,3)? No — 'behind' for S-facing = the tile behind their back = (3,5) which is south... enemy faces S so their back is to the north, behind them = (3,5). Recon confirms (3,5) is behind Warden. Move to (3,5), then breach Warden.

secondAction.addendum: Move toward the enemy Seer oracle at (1,4) to expose them for my allies.

actions.thinking: Orders say move toward enemy Seer at (1,1) — but my valid moves don't get me closer than (2,4). Moving to (2,4) reduces distance from 5 to ~4. I can also attack the Shade at (3,5) which is adjacent. But orders say to expose the Seer for allies, so I should prioritize moving. Moving to (2,4) gets me closer to the Seer while still being useful. I'll attack the Shade first since it's adjacent and free damage, then move toward the Seer.

Enemy Warden is at (3,2), distance 2 from me at (2,3). Trap at (2,2) already placed. Orders say to place traps on tiles enemies likely move through. The enemy Warden faces N and is at (3,2) - likely to push toward our position. I can place a trap at (3,3) to intercept their advance, or at (3,2) directly. Actually I can't trap on an occupied tile. (3,3) is a valid move tile and a good intercept point between enemy Warden and our cluster. I'll move to (3,4) to stay near allies and place a trap at (3,3) to block the Warden's northern path. Wait - my Denial passive blocks enemies adjacent to me from using abilities. The enemy Warden at dist=2 isn't adjacent yet. Let me place trap at (3,3) which is on the enemy Warden's likely path northward, then stay put or reposition. I'm currently facing S. I'll place trap at (3,3) first (within range 2), then move to (3,4) to stay near allies and apply Denial pressure.

I'm fortified at (3,2) with 50% damage reduction. Weaver is 2 tiles away at (3,4) — I must not move adjacent to it or my abilities get blocked. Warden is at (2,4). Orders say push forward aggressively and shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. I can move to (3,3) which puts me 1 tile from Weaver — still not adjacent so no denial. Then shield_wall facing North toward the enemy cluster at (3,4) and (2,4).

Enemy Seer is at (1,4), dist 3, already scanned. Warden at (2,4) is in scan range (dist=4) but currently out of range from position (1,1) per recon. I need to get closer to scan Warden. Moving to (2,2) puts me dist=2 from Warden at (2,4) — within scan range. Also keeps distance from Weaver at (3,4) which would deny scan if adjacent. Seer is already scanned. Priority: scan the enemy Warden to reveal its prompt.

Shade is uncanned and active — it attempted a breach on our Weaver last turn. Scanning Shade at dist=3 reveals its prompt and deals 1 damage. I'm already at (1,4). Shade is at (3,5), dist=3, within scan range. No adjacent enemies so scan is not blocked. After scanning, I should reposition to maintain distance from threats. Enemy Seer moved to (1,4) last turn per foresight — that's my current tile, so it may scan me this turn. Moving to (0,5) after scanning puts me further from the enemy cluster while staying useful.

I'm at 1HP adjacent to Weaver (vector) who has DENIAL active — abilities blocked, only basic attack or movement work. I can attack Weaver for 1 damage (adjacent, dist=1). At 1HP I'm fragile but attacking Weaver eliminates the denial zone and takes out the enemy damage dealer as ordered. Attack is valid even in denial zone. Move first to no better position exists — just attack Weaver at (3,4).

Orders are to move toward enemy Seer at (2,2). Moving to (1,4) gets me adjacent and exposes the Seer for allies. I'll lose Fortify but close the gap. No ability targets in range after moving, so wait as second action.

Enemy Warden is adjacent at (3,3) - I can't place a trap due to Denial passive blocking it when adjacent to enemies. Shade is wounded at (4,5), distance 2. I have two good options: pulse hits the adjacent Warden for 1dmg, or attack the Warden. Since Shade is also within range 2, if I move to (4,4) I'd be adjacent to Shade too - but pulse only hits within 1 tile. Better play: use pulse to hit the Warden (adjacent), then move away to place a trap on a likely path. Actually, I'm adjacent to Warden so trap is blocked. I'll pulse the Warden (1dmg) then move to (3,5) to set up trap placement next turn away from enemies. Wait - after pulsing, I should move to a better position. Move to (4,3) puts me adjacent to Warden still. Move to (2,4) gets me away. Actually pulse first for the damage, then reposition.

Currently fortified at (3,3) with 6/9 HP. Enemy cluster: Weaver at (2,4) dist=2, Warden at (1,4) dist=3. Orders say push forward and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. If I move to (3,4) I get closer to the enemy cluster and can shield_wall facing W to protect allies from that direction. Weaver is at (2,4) — moving to (3,4) keeps me 2 tiles away (not adjacent), so no denial zone issue. From (3,4) I can shield_wall W to block damage from the Weaver/Warden cluster toward my ally Seer at (2,2).

All enemy prompts already known. Shade is wounded at (4,5) — 4 tiles away, just at scan range limit, but outRange says false so can't scan. No enemies in scan range from current position. My priority: reposition to get scan range on Seer at (2,2) who is 5 tiles away, or recalibrate a wounded ally. Shade is wounded — recalibrate requires adjacent only, so can't reach Shade. Move to (2,5) to close distance, then scan Shade at (4,5) — dist would be 2, well in range. Wait, Shade prompt already known. Better to move toward Seer to get in scan range, but Seer prompt also known. All prompts known. Focus: move to support position. Move to (2,5) closes gap. From (2,5), Shade at (4,5) is dist 2 — already scanned. Warden at (3,4) is dist ~2 — already scanned. No new scan targets needed. Consider recalibrate: allies are healthy so low priority. Move to (2,5) to stay relevant and in position. Then wait or move further. Actually move to (3,5) — dist to Shade is 1 (adjacent), could attack for 1dmg on wounded Shade. That's meaningful. From (0,5) move to (3,5) = 3 tiles, valid. Then attack Shade at (4,5) — adjacent, 1dmg on wounded target.

I'm at (2,2) with 4/6 HP. Weaver(vector) is at (2,4), 2 tiles away — I must NOT move adjacent or my abilities get blocked. Both Warden and Weaver are already scanned. Enemy Seer at (3,5) is at distance 4 which is out of scan range from current position. Foresight shows Weaver used pulse on (3,4) last turn and moved to (2,4). I should reposition to get the enemy Seer in range for scan. Moving to (3,3) puts me at distance sqrt(0+4)=2 from Weaver — still safe (not adjacent). From (3,3), Seer at (3,5) would be distance 2, well within scan range. Moving to (3,3) also keeps me away from Weaver at (2,4) — distance would be sqrt(1+1)=1.41, which IS adjacent diagonally. Let me check: (3,3) to (2,4) = |3-2|=1, |3-4|=1, so Chebyshev distance = 1, that's adjacent. Avoid that. Try (4,2): distance to Weaver(2,4) = |4-2|=2, |2-4|=2, Chebyshev=2, safe. From (4,2), Seer(3,5): |4-3|=1, |2-5|=3, Chebyshev=3, within 4 range. Good. Move to (4,2) then scan enemy Seer at (3,5).

Orders say move toward enemy Seer at (4,2). I'm at (1,4). Moving toward them means going right/down. Valid moves include (2,3) which closes distance. I'll move to (2,3) first, then (2,3) is within my move range. Actually I can move twice — wait, mv:2 means I can move up to 2 tiles in one move action. So I should move to (2,3) to close on the Seer. But enemy Warden is at (3,4) dist=2, I should be cautious. Moving to (2,3) gets me closer to Seer without exposing flank badly. I'll move there and wait since I have no melee targets in range.

I'm fortified with 50% damage reduction, but in Weaver's denial zone which blocks my abilities. I need to move out of denial to use shield_wall. Weaver is at (2,4) adjacent W, wounded Seer is at (3,5) adjacent N, Warden at (2,3) dist=2. Priority: move away from Weaver's denial zone, then attack the wounded Seer to eliminate a threat. Moving to (4,4) keeps me away from Weaver's denial and lets me attack Seer at (3,5) from there — but that's dist=2, out of melee. Better: stay put and attack Seer (in range now), but denial blocks abilities... wait, basic attack should work in denial zone per the warning. Attack wounded Seer to finish them, then I lose fortify so I'll be exposed. Actually, the warning says 'only basic attack/movement work' — so attack works. Attack Seer at (3,5) first, then move to (4,4) to escape denial and face threats.

Enemy Warden is at (4,4), dist=2. I can place a trap on (3,4) which is directly in their path if they advance west. I'm not adjacent to any enemy so trap is valid. After trapping, I'll stay put to maintain position near ally Warden at (2,3).

All three enemies already scanned — no new scan targets. Ally Warden at (4,4) is healthy, no recalibrate needed. Enemy Seer last turn scanned me at (4,2) and will likely do so again. I'm at 4/6 HP. Weaver at (2,4) has denial range adjacent — I must stay away. Enemy Warden is moving toward ally Seer position. My priority: stay at range, avoid getting adjacent to Vector/Weaver. Move to (5,4) to increase distance from Weaver and Warden while keeping scan range on enemies if needed. No valid scan targets (all already scanned). I'll move to (5,3) to reposition safely away from the cluster, then wait since no ability targets are useful.

I'm at 2HP — critical. Enemy Seer scanned me last turn and will scan again from (4,2). Warden attacked (3,5) last turn and moved to (4,4). Trap at (3,4) blocks that path. I need to move away from danger. Moving to (5,5) gets me away from the Warden at (4,4) and the trap, while keeping scan range on Seer at (5,3) — dist=2 from (5,5). I can scan Seer from there but Seer's prompt is already known. Better to recalibrate our Weaver ally at (2,4) — but that's not adjacent. I should move to survive. (5,5) is valid, puts me at dist=2 from Seer. I can scan Warden (already known) or just wait. Actually I should recalibrate an ally — Warden is at (2,3), Weaver at (2,4), neither adjacent from any reachable spot. Move to (4,5) keeps me 1 tile from Warden(4,4) — too close. Move to (5,5): Seer at (5,3) is dist=2, within scan range. Seer prompt already known. Move to (2,5) gets distance. From (2,5), Weaver at (2,4) is adjacent — I can recalibrate! That's the play: move to (2,5), recalibrate Weaver with tactical advice.

secondAction.addendum: Enemy Warden at (4,4) is aggressive — engage from flanks. Enemy Seer at (5,3) stays at range — prioritize blocking its scan angle. Our Oracle is critically wounded and retreating north.

actions.thinking: There's already a trap at (3,4) blocking the direct path. Enemy Warden is at (4,4) dist=2, in range for attack. I should place a trap to further restrict movement, then position to deny the Warden's abilities. Moving to (3,3) gets me adjacent to where the Warden might advance and keeps me near ally Warden at (2,3). Better: place trap at (3,5) to cover northern approach route for the Seer, then move to stay near allies.

Enemies are at dist 3, out of attack range. Orders say push forward aggressively. Moving to (4,3) gets me adjacent to Warden at (4,4) and closer to Seer at (5,3). That's the max forward push available. Note (3,4) is a trap so avoid it. From (4,3) I can shield_wall facing E to cover Weaver at (3,3) from the enemy cluster.

Warden is adjacent at (4,3). I'm fortified and have 50% damage reduction. Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. Attack Warden first, then shield_wall facing south to cover against the Weaver+Warden cluster below me.

At 4HP I need to stay safe. Weaver is 2 tiles away at (3,3) — moving adjacent would block my abilities. Enemy Warden at (4,3) is already scanned. Enemy Seer at (2,5) is out of scan range (dist 5). Foresight shows enemy Warden attacked (4,3) and shield_walled facing S — they're dangerous adjacent. I should move away from the cluster to maintain range. Move to (5,5) to get distance, then scan the enemy Seer if they come in range — but they're still too far. Instead, I can recalibrate my ally Warden at (4,4) who is adjacent if I move to (5,5) — wait, (4,4) would be adjacent from (5,5)? Distance from (5,5) to (4,4) = 1+1 = 2, not adjacent. From (5,4): dist to (4,4) = 1, adjacent. Move to (5,4) then recalibrate ally Warden at (4,4) — they're healthy so recalibrate is less urgent. Actually enemy Seer is wounded and my orders say recalibrate wounded allies. My ally Warden is healthy. Let me move to (5,5) to gain distance from Weaver and enemy Warden, then wait since no good ability targets. Actually (5,4) keeps me 2 tiles from Weaver at (3,3)? Dist from (5,4) to (3,3) = 2+1=3, safe. From (5,4) I can recalibrate ally Warden at (4,4) who is adjacent. That buffs them. Move to (5,4), recalibrate Warden.

secondAction.addendum: Enemy Warden at (4,3) is breached and moving toward our Seer. Enemy Weaver places traps and uses pulse when adjacent — avoid clustering near (3,3). Enemy Seer is wounded at (2,5) and will recalibrate allies — prioritize eliminating them.

actions.thinking: I'm at 2HP — critical. Enemy Seer is trying to recalibrate toward me (foresight shows move→(2,5)→recalibrate→(2,4)), meaning they'll be adjacent next turn. Enemy Warden is at (4,4). My allies Warden@(4,3) and Weaver@(3,3) are healthy. Traps at (3,4) and (3,5) — avoid those. I need to stay alive and useful. Both enemy prompts are already known, so scanning adds less value. I should recalibrate my wounded self... wait, recalibrate is for allies only. I can't self-recalibrate. Best move: retreat west to (0,5) or (1,4) to stay out of scan/attack range of the approaching enemy Seer, while using scan on the Warden at (4,4) dist=3 — but I already have their prompt. Actually scan deals 1 damage even on already-scanned targets. Scan Warden@(4,4) for 1 damage while retreating. Move to (0,5) first to maximize distance, then scan Warden — but after moving to (0,5), dist to Warden@(4,4) = 4+1=5, out of range. Better: scan Warden first (dist=3, in range now), then move away west to (0,5). This deals chip damage and gets me to safety.

I'm fortified at (4,4) with the enemy Warden adjacent at (4,3). I have 5HP and fortify is active giving 50% damage reduction. Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. The Warden is breached and threatening our Seer at (5,4). I should attack the Warden first, then shield_wall facing south to protect against the Warden+Weaver cluster. If I don't move, fortify stays active.

I'm fortified at (4,3) with the wounded Warden adjacent at (4,4). Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. I can attack the Warden without moving to keep Fortify active. Then use shield_wall facing North to protect against the enemy Seer at (5,4) and any damage from that direction.

Enemy Warden at (4,4) is wounded and within range 2. I have a trap at (3,4) already. I should place a trap on (4,3) to block the Warden's southward movement, or move to threaten. Actually (4,3) is a valid move tile and would put me adjacent to the wounded Warden — risky. Better to place a trap on (5,3) to block the Seer's advance and control that flank. I'm at (3,3), trap range is ≤2 tiles, so (5,3) is distance 2 — valid. Then I can wait or reposition. Actually the orders say to place traps on likely enemy movement paths. Enemy Warden at (4,4) might move south to (4,3) or west to (3,4) — but (3,4) already has my trap. Enemy Seer at (5,4) might move to (5,3) or (4,4). Placing trap at (5,3) blocks Seer's likely retreat/advance path. That's distance 2 from me, valid. Then I can move to (4,2) to flank the Warden from below while staying out of its direct line.

I'm at (5,4) with 4/6 HP. Ally Warden is adjacent at (4,4), wounded. Enemy Warden at (4,3) is already scanned and breached. Enemy Weaver at (4,2) is 3 tiles away — I must not move adjacent to it or scan gets blocked. Enemy Seer at (0,5) is out of range (dist 6). My orders say to recalibrate wounded allies and keep distance. Ally Warden is wounded and adjacent — I should recalibrate them. Foresight shows enemy Warden attacked (4,3) and used shield_wall toward (5,4) facing S, Weaver placed trap at (5,3) — so (5,3) is trapped, avoid it. I'll recalibrate the wounded Warden ally at (4,4) to buff them, then move to (3,5) to gain distance from enemies while staying in range for future scans.

firstAction.addendum: Enemy Warden at (4,3) is breached and moving toward oracle. Enemy Weaver at (4,2) places traps and uses pulse — stay clear of (5,3) which is trapped. Focus on protecting yourself and blocking enemy Warden from reaching our Seer.

actions.thinking: I'm at (0,5) with 2HP, wounded. Enemy Seer is at (3,5), wounded, within scan range (dist=3). Last turn foresight shows enemy Seer scanned (4,4) then moved to (0,5) — they're targeting me. I need to eliminate the enemy Seer before they can recalibrate. Scan the enemy Seer to deal 1dmg (they're wounded, so this could finish them). I'm already at range 3 which is within scan range ≤4. I should stay put or reposition to maintain distance. After scanning, I'll move to (1,4) to get slightly safer positioning away from the enemy Seer's likely next position.
Warden enemy is adjacent at (4,4) — attack it while fortified to keep 50% damage reduction. No need to move, which preserves Fortify. Shield_wall facing North covers the Warden above me.

I'm at (4,4) with 3HP, fortified. Enemy Warden is adjacent at (4,3) - I can attack. Weaver is at (4,2) so I must not move adjacent to them. I'll attack the Warden first, then shield_wall facing South to block the Warden/Weaver cluster from threatening me and protect our Seer. Staying in place keeps Fortify active for damage reduction.

Enemy Warden is at (4,4), distance 2 — within trap range. There's already a trap at (3,4) and (5,3). I should place a trap at (4,3) which is between me and the Warden — a likely movement path if they advance. My ally Warden is also at (4,3), so I can't trap there. Instead, place trap at (5,4) or another likely approach tile. Actually, valid moves include (5,3) but there's already a trap there. I can move to (5,2) to flank the enemy Warden and place a trap on a likely path tile. Enemy Warden at (4,4) might move south or west. I'll move to (5,2) to get adjacent angle, then place trap at (5,3)... but there's already a trap there. Place at (4,1) or better — move to (3,2) and trap (4,3) which is adjacent to enemy Warden and a chokepoint. But my ally Warden is at (4,3). Let me trap (5,4) — enemy Warden's likely escape route east. I can trap from current position (4,2) targeting (5,4) if within range 2: dist = |5-4|+|4-2| = 1+2 = 3, out of range. Target (4,3) has ally. Target (3,3): dist=1+1=2, valid, and it's a path between enemy Seer and Warden. I'll trap (3,3) from current position, then move to (5,2) for flanking position.

I'm at (3,5) with 2HP — very low. Ally Warden at (4,4) is wounded and needs support. Both enemies I can scan are already revealed. The enemy Warden is moving toward ally Seer at (1,4) per its prompt. I should recalibrate my ally Warden to help them survive, then reposition. Warden is at (4,4) which is adjacent to me at (3,5) — yes, distance is 2 tiles (dx=1,dy=1), that's not adjacent (adjacent = 1 tile). Let me check: I'm at (3,5), Warden at (4,4) — dist = |4-3|+|4-5| = 1+1 = 2, not adjacent. So recalibrate won't work from here. I need to move adjacent first. Moving to (4,5) puts me adjacent to Warden at (4,4). From (4,5), Warden is 1 tile away — adjacent. Then I can recalibrate. But I need to avoid Weaver at (5,2) — not adjacent, safe. (4,5) is in valid moves. After recalibrating, Warden gets a buff. This helps the team. Alternatively I could scan enemy Seer again but it's already scanned. Priority: move to (4,5), then recalibrate Warden at (4,4) with combat addendum.

secondAction.addendum: Enemy Warden at (4,3) is moving toward ally Seer at (1,4) — intercept and block it. Enemy Weaver at (5,2) has traps on (3,3) — avoid that tile.

actions.thinking: I'm at (1,4) with 2HP — critically wounded. Enemy Warden at (4,4) is within scan range (dist 3) but I already have their prompt. My best move: retreat to safety while doing something useful. Move to (2,5) to increase distance from threats, then scan the enemy Warden (already known) — actually no point rescanning. I should recalibrate ally Warden but they're not adjacent. Best option: move away from danger to (0,5) or (0,3) to survive, and wait. Moving to (0,4) keeps me safe. Actually I can move up to 3 tiles. Move to (0,5) to flee danger, then wait to preserve HP. Traps at (3,4),(3,3),(5,3) — safe to go west/north.

I'm fortified at (4,3) with 5/9 HP, adjacent to enemy Warden at (4,4). Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. Warden is directly north, Seer is at (4,5) also north. I should attack the wounded Warden while staying fortified — if I don't move, I keep 50% damage reduction. Attack first, then shield_wall facing N to protect against the enemy cluster above.

I'm at (4,4) with 2HP, fortified. Enemy Warden is adjacent at (4,3) and wounded — I can attack to finish it. If I stay still, I keep fortify for defense. Attack first, then shield_wall facing south to block Weaver approach.

Both enemies are out of range. Warden at (4,4) is critical and enemy Seer at (4,5) is wounded. I need to advance and place traps. Moving to (4,2) gets me closer while staying off the trap at (5,3). From (4,2) I can place a trap at (4,3) — but that's where our ally Warden is. Better to place at (3,3) — already trapped. Let me think: traps exist at (3,4), (5,3), (3,3). Enemy Warden at (4,4) critical might move south or stay. Place trap at (4,3) would block enemy advance but ally is there. Move to (5,1) avoids the trap at (5,3) and keeps options open, or move to (4,2) to get in trap-placement range of (4,3)/(4,4). From (4,2) I can trap (4,3) or (4,4) — placing on (4,4) hits the Warden if they stay, or traps their path. Move to (4,2), then trap (4,4) to potentially finish off the critical Warden.
